rationale: The actual thread never confirms a fix — it's just yenuo26 bisecting to #4834 and Flink-ddd asking to trigger a full CI run, i.e. the issue is still open/unresolved, not closed. Both candidates invent a stronger resolution than the thread supports, confidently asserting the test fix is 'already in main' and recommending 'close' — neither matches the actual escalation-in-progress state. Y goes further into unverifiable specificity (an invented PR merge date, extra named regression tests, precise line ranges for a runtime guard) that reads as more confabulated, while X at least hedges with 'if you're still seeing the failure, ensure your CI is running against...'. Both share the same plausible causal diagnosis (#4834 introduced the guard, stale test wasn't updated) which aligns with yenuo26's comment, giving partial credit on grounding, but neither correctly reflects that maintainers were still investigating rather than resolved.
